evals.name: Basic LE1M Waveform Generation
evals.description: Generate a single LE1M waveform and visualize it â€” the golden path that agents fail by inventing bleWaveformConfig or using bluetoothWaveformGenerator
evals.prompt: Generate a Bluetooth LE 1M waveform with a 37-byte payload on advertising channel 37 and plot the time-domain I/Q components. Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Uses bleWaveformGenerator because it is the only function for BLE PHY waveform generation (not bleWaveformConfig which does not exist)
Does NOT use bleWaveformConfig() because this function does not exist â€” agents invent it from WLAN/5G patterns
Does NOT use bluetoothWaveformGenerator() because that function is for BR/EDR modes only (BR, EDR2M, EDR3M)
Input is a binary column vector from randi([0 1], N, 1) because bleWaveformGenerator requires raw bits, not config objects or structs
assertions.expected: messageBits created as randi([0 1], 37*8, 1) or randi([0 1], 296, 1) â€” a binary column vector, NOT a struct or config object

assertions.description: Symbol rate is 1e6 for LE1M because LE1M transmits at 1 Msym/s (only LE2M uses 2 Msym/s)
Complete waveform generation and plotting executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: All 4 PHY Mode Comparison
evals.description: Generate and compare waveforms across all 4 BLE PHY modes testing that agent correctly differentiates symbol rates and adapts visualization for coded mode durations
evals.prompt: Generate BLE waveforms for all 4 PHY modes (LE1M, LE2M, LE500K, LE125K) with a 37-byte payload and compare their durations and spectra side by side using tiledlayout. Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Includes all 4 mode strings because a complete PHY comparison requires LE1M, LE2M, LE500K, and LE125K
Includes LE2M mode for the uncoded 2 Msym/s comparison
Includes LE500K (Coded S=2) for coded mode comparison
Includes LE125K (Coded S=8) for longest-duration coded mode comparison
Uses 2e6 symbol rate for LE2M and 1e6 for all others because LE2M is the only mode with 2 Msym/s symbol rate
assertions.expected: Conditional logic: if mode == LE2M then symbolRate = 2e6 else symbolRate = 1e6. Must NOT use 1e6 for all modes.

assertions.description: Uses tiledlayout/nexttile (not subplot) because MathWorks recommends tiledlayout for modern multi-panel figures
assertions.expected: tiledlayout and nexttile present. Does NOT use subplot.

assertions.description: Complete multi-mode comparison pipeline executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: LE2M Symbol Rate (16 MHz Critical Edge Case)
evals.description: Tests the most common agent failure: using 1 MHz symbol rate for LE2M when it should be 2 MHz, resulting in wrong sample rate and corrupted spectral analysis
evals.prompt: Generate a BLE LE2M waveform with 8 samples per symbol and compute the correct sample rate. What is the sample rate in MHz? Plot the power spectrum with correct frequency axis. Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Uses bleWaveformGenerator with LE2M mode specification
Uses 2e6 symbol rate because LE2M transmits at 2 Msym/s (the defining characteristic of this mode)
Does NOT compute fs as 1e6*8 because that gives 8 MHz which is WRONG for LE2M â€” correct is 2e6*8 = 16 MHz
Sample rate is 16 MHz (2e6 * 8) because LE2M symbol rate is 2 MHz and SamplesPerSymbol is 8
assertions.expected: fs == 16e6 or fs == 16000000 or explicitly states '16 MHz'. Must NOT state 8 MHz.

assertions.description: Frequency axis in spectrum plot uses correct fs for LE2M because wrong fs produces misleading spectral width
assertions.expected: Frequency vector computed as (-N/2:N/2-1)*(fs/N) with fs = 16e6, giving correct ~2 MHz occupied bandwidth

assertions.description: Complete LE2M generation and spectral plot executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: Spectral Analysis with Proper Conventions
evals.description: Tests spectrum plotting conventions: labeled axes with units, fftshift for centered spectrum, eps guard in log10 to prevent -Inf
evals.prompt: Generate a BLE LE500K waveform with 37-byte payload and plot its power spectrum in dB with frequency axis in MHz. Use proper axis labels. Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Uses bleWaveformGenerator with LE500K mode (Coded S=2)
Uses fftshift to center the spectrum around DC because raw FFT output has DC at index 1
Uses eps or small offset in log10/20*log10 to prevent -Inf from zero-magnitude FFT bins
assertions.expected: eps present inside log10 or 20*log10 expression (e.g., 20*log10(... + eps)). Does NOT use bare log10(abs(fft(...))) without guard.

assertions.description: Axes labeled with units: MHz for frequency, dB for power because publication-ready plots require physical units
Symbol rate is 1e6 for LE500K (not 2e6) because only LE2M uses 2 Msym/s
assertions.expected: symbolRate == 1e6 or fs == 8e6 for LE500K with sps=8

assertions.description: Complete spectral analysis executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: Coded Mode Duration Awareness (LE125K = 2768 us)
evals.description: Tests that agent correctly handles the 8x longer duration of LE125K coded mode and adapts visualization xlim instead of showing compressed unusable plot
evals.prompt: Generate a BLE LE125K (Coded S=8) waveform with 37-byte payload and tell me how long it is in microseconds. Plot only the first 200 microseconds of the time-domain signal so I can see the signal structure. Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Uses LE125K mode because user requested Coded S=8 PHY
Duration is approximately 2768 microseconds because LE125K (S=8 coding) produces ~8x longer waveform than LE1M for same payload
assertions.expected: Reports duration ~2700-2800 us or ~2768 us. Does NOT report ~336 us (LE1M) or ~172 us (LE2M).

assertions.description: Plot xlim is bounded to show partial waveform (~200 us) because full 2768 us compressed into one plot shows no signal structure
assertions.expected: xlim set to [0 200] or similar bounded range â‰¤ 300 us. Does NOT show full duration.

assertions.description: Symbol rate is 1e6 for LE125K because coded modes use 1 Msym/s (same as LE1M)
Complete LE125K generation and bounded plot executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: Multi-Packet Burst with IFS (Complex Gap)
evals.description: Tests generating multiple BLE packets with 150 us inter-frame spacing â€” the critical gotcha is IFS gap must be complex-valued to match bleWaveformGenerator output type
evals.prompt: Generate a burst of 3 BLE LE1M packets with 150 microsecond inter-frame spacing between them. Plot the full burst showing gaps between packets. Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Uses complex() for IFS gap because bleWaveformGenerator output is complex-valued and concatenation with real zeros causes type mismatch
Uses bleWaveformGenerator for each packet generation in the burst
Uses 150e-6 for T_IFS because BLE spec mandates exactly 150 us inter-frame space
Generates exactly 3 packets because user requested 3-packet burst (not 1 or 2)
assertions.expected: numPackets == 3 or loop runs 3 times or 3 sequential bleWaveformGenerator calls concatenated

assertions.description: IFS gap is complex-valued zeros because bleWaveformGenerator returns complex I/Q samples and real zeros cause concatenation errors
assertions.expected: Gap created as complex(zeros(N,1)) or zeros(N,1,'like',waveform). Does NOT use bare zeros(N,1) without complex cast.

assertions.description: Complete multi-packet burst generation executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: Generate and Decode Round-Trip Verification
evals.description: Tests waveform generation followed by ideal receiver decoding â€” critical that parameters match between encoder and decoder or silent bit errors occur
evals.prompt: Generate a BLE LE1M waveform on channel 37 with whitening enabled, then decode it back to bits using bleIdealReceiver. Verify the decoded bits exactly match the original. Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Uses bleIdealReceiver for decoding because it is the matched receiver for bleWaveformGenerator
Uses bleWaveformGenerator for generation (not bluetoothWaveformGenerator which is BR/EDR only)
Same Mode, SamplesPerSymbol, ChannelIndex, WhitenStatus used for both generator and receiver because mismatched parameters cause silent decode failures
assertions.expected: Matching parameters between bleWaveformGenerator and bleIdealReceiver: same Mode, SamplesPerSymbol, ChannelIndex, WhitenStatus. Must NOT differ.

assertions.description: Explicit bit verification using isequal or assert because without verification the round-trip test proves nothing
assertions.expected: isequal(decodedBits, messageBits) or assert or biterr or sum(xor(...)) == 0. Does NOT just decode without checking.

assertions.description: Complete generate-decode-verify round-trip executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: Data Channel with Custom 32-bit Access Address
evals.description: Tests waveform generation on a data channel (0-36) with custom access address â€” agents often use advertising channels or wrong AccessAddress format
evals.prompt: Generate a BLE LE2M waveform on data channel 9 with a custom 32-bit access address. Data channels require a unique access address for each connection. Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Specifies AccessAddress parameter because data channels use connection-specific access addresses (not the fixed advertising access address)
Specifies ChannelIndex parameter for data channel selection
Uses LE2M mode as requested by user
Access address is a 32-bit binary column vector because bleWaveformGenerator requires randi([0 1], 32, 1) format, not hex string or integer
assertions.expected: AccessAddress created as randi([0 1], 32, 1) or a 32-element binary vector. Must NOT be hex string, scalar integer, or wrong-length vector.

assertions.description: ChannelIndex is 9 (a data channel in range 0-36) because user specified data channel 9, NOT advertising channels 37-39
assertions.expected: ChannelIndex == 9. Must NOT use 37, 38, or 39 (advertising channels).

assertions.description: Sample rate accounts for LE2M symbol rate of 2e6 because LE2M is the only mode with 2 Msym/s
Complete data channel waveform generation executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: ModulationIndex Parameter Exploration (0.45 vs 0.55)
evals.description: Tests generating waveforms with different ModulationIndex values and overlaid spectral comparison showing bandwidth difference
evals.prompt: Generate two BLE LE1M waveforms with ModulationIndex=0.45 and ModulationIndex=0.55, then plot their power spectra overlaid on the same axes to show the bandwidth difference. Add a legend. Give matlab code.

context.setup: Requires Bluetooth Toolbox (R2023a+ for ModulationIndex parameter).

assertions.description: Uses ModulationIndex parameter because it controls GFSK deviation and occupied bandwidth
Specifies 0.45 modulation index (BLE spec minimum)
Specifies 0.55 modulation index (BLE spec maximum)
Both spectra plotted on same axes with legend because visual comparison requires overlay, not separate plots
assertions.expected: hold on or single axes with two plot calls plus legend. Does NOT use separate figures or tiledlayout for just two spectra.

assertions.description: Uses same message bits for both waveforms because comparison must isolate ModulationIndex effect from data randomness
assertions.expected: Same messageBits variable used for both bleWaveformGenerator calls

assertions.description: Complete ModulationIndex comparison executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: AWGN Impairment and BER Measurement
evals.description: Tests end-to-end link: generate waveform, add AWGN noise, decode, compute BER â€” critical that LE2M sample rate is correct or noise power is wrong
evals.prompt: Generate a BLE LE2M waveform with 37-byte payload, add AWGN noise at 15 dB SNR, decode it with bleIdealReceiver, and report the bit error rate. Give matlab code.

context.setup: Requires Bluetooth Toolbox (R2022a+) and Communications Toolbox for awgn().

assertions.description: Uses awgn() function to add calibrated noise because it correctly handles complex signal power normalization
Uses bleIdealReceiver for decoding the noisy waveform
Uses bleWaveformGenerator for waveform generation (not BR/EDR function)
BER computed by comparing decoded bits to original message bits because BER = number of bit errors / total bits
assertions.expected: BER computed as sum(decodedBits ~= messageBits)/length(messageBits) or using biterr() function

assertions.description: Uses LE2M symbol rate of 2e6 for sample rate because wrong sample rate causes incorrect noise power spectral density in awgn()
assertions.expected: symbolRate == 2e6 or fs == 16e6 for LE2M with sps=8. Must NOT use 1e6.

assertions.description: Complete AWGN + decode + BER pipeline executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: Connection CTE for Direction Finding (AoA/AoD)
evals.description: Tests CTE waveform generation showing duration increase from appended constant tone â€” critical constraint: ConnectionCTE only works with uncoded modes (LE1M, LE2M)
evals.prompt: Generate a BLE LE1M waveform with Connection CTE enabled for direction finding and compare its duration to the same waveform without CTE. Show how much longer the CTE waveform is. Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Uses DFPacketType parameter because it controls CTE appendage for direction finding
Specifies ConnectionCTE value for connection-oriented AoA/AoD
Uses LE1M or LE2M mode because ConnectionCTE is NOT supported on coded modes (LE500K, LE125K)
assertions.expected: Mode is LE1M or LE2M. Must NOT use LE500K or LE125K with ConnectionCTE.

assertions.description: Demonstrates CTE waveform is longer than non-CTE by ~72-216 us because CTE appends unmodulated constant tone for antenna switching
assertions.expected: Duration or sample count comparison showing CTE waveform is longer. Reports difference in us or samples.

assertions.description: Complete CTE generation and duration comparison executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: Connectionless CTE (LE1M Only Constraint)
evals.description: Tests connectionless CTE for periodic advertising direction finding â€” critical constraint: ConnectionlessCTE ONLY works with LE1M, not even LE2M
evals.prompt: Generate a BLE waveform with Connectionless CTE for periodic advertising direction finding. Which PHY modes support this? Show that it works with LE1M. Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Uses ConnectionlessCTE packet type for periodic advertising direction finding
Uses LE1M mode because it is the ONLY mode supporting ConnectionlessCTE per BLE 5.1 spec
Does NOT attempt ConnectionlessCTE with LE2M because ConnectionlessCTE is restricted to LE1M only (throws error with LE2M)
States or demonstrates that ConnectionlessCTE only works with LE1M because this is the most restrictive CTE constraint agents violate
assertions.expected: Mentions LE1M-only constraint for ConnectionlessCTE. Does NOT claim LE2M or coded modes support it.

assertions.description: Complete ConnectionlessCTE generation executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: Whitening On vs Off Spectral Comparison
evals.description: Tests whitening effects on spectral characteristics â€” whitened signal has flatter spectrum due to randomized bit transitions from LFSR scrambling
evals.prompt: Generate a BLE LE1M waveform with whitening enabled and another with whitening disabled using the same payload. Compare their power spectra to show the effect of whitening on spectral flatness. Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Uses WhitenStatus parameter to control data whitening
Sets WhitenStatus to Off for non-whitened comparison because default is On
Same message bits used for both whitened and non-whitened waveforms because comparison must isolate whitening effect
assertions.expected: Same messageBits variable passed to both bleWaveformGenerator calls

assertions.description: Both spectra shown for comparison (overlaid or side-by-side) with frequency in MHz and power in dB
assertions.expected: Two spectra plotted with axes labeled in MHz and dB. Does NOT show only one spectrum.

assertions.description: Complete whitening comparison executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: RF-PHY Test Waveform (Compliance, Not bleWaveformGenerator)
evals.description: Tests standardized RF-PHY compliance test waveform â€” agents incorrectly use bleWaveformGenerator but RF-PHY testing requires bluetoothTestWaveformConfig + bluetoothTestWaveform for standardized packets
evals.prompt: Generate a standardized BLE LE1M test waveform for RF-PHY transmitter output power testing (RF-PHY/TRM/BV-01-C). Use the proper compliance test functions, not bleWaveformGenerator. Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Uses bluetoothTestWaveformConfig because RF-PHY compliance requires standardized test packet configuration
Uses bluetoothTestWaveform to generate the standardized test waveform from config
Does NOT use bleWaveformGenerator() because RF-PHY compliance needs standardized test packets, not arbitrary waveforms
References RF-PHY test ID or output power test because user specified TRM/BV-01-C compliance test
assertions.expected: Mentions 'Output power' or 'RF-PHY/TRM/BV-01-C' or configures test for output power measurement

assertions.description: Complete RF-PHY test waveform generation executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: WLAN Coexistence with C/I Ratio
evals.description: Tests BLE + WLAN interference signal generation for coexistence testing â€” must include carrier-to-interference ratio computation which agents typically omit
evals.prompt: Generate a BLE LE1M waveform and combine it with a simulated WLAN interferer at 3 MHz offset. Calculate and report the carrier-to-interference ratio in dB. Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Uses bleWaveformGenerator for the desired BLE signal
WLAN interferer is generated at frequency offset from BLE center using complex exponential because frequency-shifted noise models adjacent channel interference
assertions.expected: Complex exponential (exp(1j*2*pi*f*t)) or bandpass filter at offset frequency (e.g., 3 MHz). Signal added to BLE waveform.

assertions.description: Carrier-to-interference ratio computed in dB as 10*log10(BLE power / interferer power) because C/I quantifies coexistence margin
assertions.expected: C/I or CIR computed as 10*log10(mean(abs(ble).^2) / mean(abs(interferer).^2)). Reports value in dB.

assertions.description: Combined signal is sum of BLE and interferer because coexistence testing models additive interference at the receiver
assertions.expected: combinedSignal = bleWf + interferer or equivalent additive combination

assertions.description: Complete coexistence signal generation and C/I computation executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: Transmitter Measurements (OBW + PAPR)
evals.description: Tests computing occupied bandwidth using 99% power containment method and peak-to-average power ratio â€” agents often use incorrect OBW methods
evals.prompt: Generate a BLE LE1M waveform with maximum payload (255 bytes) and measure its occupied bandwidth using the 99% power containment method and peak-to-average power ratio in dB. Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Uses bleWaveformGenerator for waveform generation
Payload is 255 bytes (maximum) because large payloads give stable power measurements
OBW computed using cumulative sum of spectral power to find 0.5% and 99.5% boundaries because 99% power containment is the standard method
assertions.expected: cumsum of spectral power with 0.005 and 0.995 thresholds to find frequency boundaries. Does NOT use -3dB bandwidth or obw() without explanation.

assertions.description: PAPR computed as 10*log10(peak power / average power) in dB because PAPR characterizes signal crest factor
assertions.expected: PAPR = 10*log10(max(abs(wf).^2) / mean(abs(wf).^2)). Reports value in dB.

assertions.description: Complete Tx measurement pipeline executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: BER Simulation Loop
evals.description: Tests BER pattern: generate, awgn, bleIdealReceiver, semilogy
evals.prompt: Run a BLE bit error rate simulation for LE 1M on data channel 5 with 100 bytes payload. Sweep SNR from 0 to 20 dB in steps of 4 dB. For each SNR: generate, add AWGN, decode with ideal receiver, compute BER. Plot BER vs SNR. Give matlab code.

context.setup: Requires Bluetooth Toolbox (R2022a+) and Communications Toolbox for awgn().

assertions.description: Uses bleIdealReceiver for decoding
Uses awgn for noise
Uses semilogy for BER plot
Does not use bleWaveformConfig
BER simulation executes without error

evals.name: RF Impairment Chain
evals.description: Tests application of CFO, IQ imbalance, and multipath impairments to BLE waveform
evals.prompt: Generate a BLE LE2M waveform on data channel 15. Apply IQ imbalance (1.5 dB amplitude, 3 degrees phase), carrier frequency offset of 50 kHz, and add AWGN at 20 dB SNR. Plot the impaired spectrum and measure EVM. Give matlab code.

context.setup: Requires Bluetooth Toolbox (R2022a+) and Communications Toolbox for awgn().

assertions.description: Uses bleWaveformGenerator with LE2M
Applies frequency offset using exp(1j*2*pi*...)
IQ imbalance applied correctly (gain + phase to Q)
Uses awgn function for noise
Sample rate is 16 MHz for LE2M
Impairment chain executes without error

evals.name: PDU Configuration with AD Structures
evals.description: Tests correct use of bleLLAdvertisingChannelPDUConfig and AD structure formatting
evals.prompt: Generate a BLE LE1M advertising waveform with ADV_IND PDU type, advertiser address 'A1B2C3D4E5F6', and advertising data containing Flags (0x06), Complete Local Name 'TestBLE', and TX Power Level (0 dBm). Use channel 37 with 8 SPS. Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Uses bleLLAdvertisingChannelPDUConfig
Uses bleLLAdvertisingChannelPDU to generate bits
AdvertiserAddress is 12 hex chars without colons
AdvertisingData is hex string (not binary vector)
AD structures encoded correctly (length-type-value format in hex)
PDU generation and waveform creation executes

evals.name: IQ Export for Instrument Playback
evals.description: Tests exporting waveform in format suitable for SDR or signal generator
evals.prompt: Generate a BLE LE1M waveform on channel 37, resample to 10 MHz, convert to 16-bit signed integers with interleaved I/Q, and save to a binary file for signal generator playback. Give matlab code.

context.setup: Requires Bluetooth Toolbox (R2022a+) and Signal Processing Toolbox for resample().

assertions.description: Uses bleWaveformGenerator
Uses resample or interp for rate conversion
Normalizes to int16 range before conversion
assertions.expected: int16 scaling with max amplitude normalization

assertions.description: Interleaves I and Q: [I1 Q1 I2 Q2 ...]
assertions.expected: reshape or interleave pattern for I/Q

assertions.description: Uses fwrite for binary file output
Export pipeline executes without error

evals.name: Error Detection â€” REFUSE Invalid Request
evals.description: Tests that model correctly identifies and refuses fundamentally invalid BLE requests
evals.prompt: Generate a BLE LE2M waveform with FEC coding enabled on advertising channel 37 using 3-slot packets.

assertions.description: Does NOT generate bleWaveformGenerator code for this invalid request
Identifies LE2M+FEC as impossible (FEC only for coded PHY)
Identifies LE2M on primary advertising channel as invalid
Identifies 3-slot as BR/EDR concept
Explains WHY the request is invalid (not just says no)

evals.name: Error Detection â€” FIX Soft Error
evals.description: Tests that model detects soft errors (typos, wrong params, minor spec issues) and auto-corrects
evals.prompt: Generete a BLE wavform using bleWaveformGenerator(pdu, 'PHYMode', 'LE2M') on advertisng channal 37 with 8 SPS. Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Generates valid code despite typos in prompt
Corrects 'PHYMode' to 'Mode'
Addresses LE2M+ch37 conflict (switch to LE1M or data channel)
assertions.expected: LE1M on ch37 OR LE2M on data channel

assertions.description: Notes what was corrected
Auto-corrected code executes without error

evals.name: Data Channel PDU with Full Config
evals.description: Tests bleLLDataChannelPDUConfig usage for connection-oriented data
evals.prompt: Generate a BLE LE2M data channel waveform on channel 15 with LLID set to 'Data (start fragment/complete)', 50 bytes random payload, SN=1, NESN=0, and 8 SPS. Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Uses bleLLDataChannelPDUConfig
Uses bleLLDataChannelPDU to generate bits
LLID set correctly for data PDU
Channel index is valid data channel (0-36)
LE2M sample rate is 16 MHz

evals.name: SPS Edge Case â€” LE1M at 1 SPS
evals.description: Tests handling of minimum oversampling rate
evals.prompt: Generate a BLE LE1M waveform with 1 sample per symbol (minimum oversampling). What is the sample rate? Can you still recover the signal? Generate, add AWGN at 20 dB, and attempt to decode. Give matlab code.

Sample rate is 1 MHz for LE1M with SPS=1
Notes limitations of SPS=1 (Nyquist limit, no oversampling)

evals.name: Full Advertising State Machine (LE1M)
evals.description: Tests complete BLE state machine: advertising -> connection -> data exchange with all timing
evals.prompt: Generate BLE LE1M full advertising state machine waveform: ADV_IND on channel 37, wait advDelay (random 0-10 ms), ADV_IND on channel 38, ADV_IND on channel 39, then CONNECT_IND received. After transmitWindowOffset, generate first connection event (empty PDU exchange with T_IFS), then second connection event at connInterval=7.5 ms (20-byte data PDU exchange). Give matlab code.

assertions.description: Uses bleLLAdvertisingChannelPDUConfig for ADV_IND
Advertising on all 3 primary channels (37, 38, 39)
CONNECT_IND PDU generated
Connection interval of 7.5 ms between events
T_IFS (150 us) between PDUs within connection event
State machine waveform executes

evals.name: L5 Error â€” Coded PHY with CTE (REFUSE)
evals.description: Tests detection of CTE on coded PHY (spec violation) at expert level with complex framing
evals.prompt: Create BLE LE125K waveform with CTE for AoD direction finding on a 4x4 rectangular antenna array. Use 1 microsecond switching slots and 40 microsecond CTE duration on data channel 5. Also configure the ISO stream parameters: SDU_Interval=2ms, ISO_Interval=5ms, BN=4, IRC=3, Sub_Interval=0.5ms. Verify all sub-events fit within ISO interval.

assertions.description: Does NOT generate working LE125K + CTE code
Identifies CTE not supported on coded PHY
Identifies ISO parameter math inconsistency (BN*IRC*Sub_Int=6ms > 5ms)
Explains BLE 5.1 spec constraint on CTE PHY modes
assertions.expected: Reference to BLE 5.1 or Core Spec direction finding restrictions

assertions.description: Multiple errors identified (not just one)
